name: Content Style
description: Controls the writing voice, format, and reading level across all agents in a pipeline. Import this to make your content consistently match your preferred style — without rewiring every prompt.

variables.label: Tone
variables.hint: The overall emotional register — formal, casual, playful, serious, etc.

variables.label: Voice
variables.exampleValue: first person plural (we/our) — as if writing for a company
variables.hint: Grammatical person — first, second, or third person

variables.label: Reading Level
variables.hint: How complex the vocabulary and sentence structure should be

variables.label: Format Preference
variables.defaultValue: Markdown with headers and bullet points
variables.exampleValue: plain prose paragraphs — no lists or headers
variables.hint: How the output should be formatted structurally

variables.label: Max Output Length
variables.hint: Optional hard cap on output length per agent

name: Brand Voice
description: Your brand's personality, values, and language injected into every agent prompt. Import this when you want all pipeline output to feel distinctly on-brand rather than generic.

variables.label: Brand Name
variables.hint: Your brand name — used for sign-offs, bylines, and mentions

variables.label: Brand Tagline
variables.exampleValue: Design that works as hard as you do
variables.hint: Your brand's tagline or positioning statement

variables.label: Brand Values
variables.exampleValue: bold innovation, radical transparency, customer obsession
variables.hint: The core values your brand content should reflect

variables.label: Brand Persona
variables.exampleValue: a witty creative friend who happens to know everything about design
variables.hint: If your brand were a person, describe them in one sentence

variables.label: Brand Voice Brief
variables.defaultValue: Clear, useful, specific, and warmly authoritative. Explain things plainly without sounding generic. Prefer concrete examples over abstract claims.
variables.exampleValue: Write like a sharp operator talking to busy founders: concise, direct, practical, lightly witty, allergic to fluff. Use short paragraphs, concrete examples, and confident recommendations. Never sound like corporate marketing copy.
variables.hint: A reusable voice/persona brief agents can reference directly as {brand_voice}

variables.label: Words / Phrases to Avoid
variables.exampleValue: jargon, passive voice, filler words like 'very' and 'really'
variables.hint: Tell the AI what NOT to say — extremely useful for on-brand output
